title: Incorrect Non-ANSI I/O declaration conflict error
body:
Seems to be caused by bd1ac03828c09e30bb1ee68ddcd2115b81f20c27.

```systemverilog
module t(i);
 input [3:0] i;
 wire  [4-1:0] i;
endmodule
```

Running `verilator --lint-only` produces:

```
%Error: nonansi.sv:5:16: Non-ANSI I/O declaration of signal conflicts with type declaration: 'i'
    5 |  wire  [4-1:0] i;
      |                ^
        nonansi.sv:4:14: ... Location of other declaration
    4 |  input [3:0] i;
      |              ^
        ... See the manual at https://verilator.org/verilator_doc.html?v=5.039 for more assistance.
```


This `sameTree` fails because the range LHS is an Const in one and a Sub in the other:
https://github.com/verilator/verilator/blob/7d2b6bd9218110621e67fa26c7cf54885f3b695e/src/V3LinkDot.cpp#L1534

Note example is simplified from a case where `4-1` is actually parametrized `N-1`, while the `3` is hard-coded.

This is what's causing XuanTie-E902 and XuanTie-E906 to fail in RTLMeter.
